title: Negotiating a Flatshare Agreement
summary: Megan and Daniel, two new flatmates, sit down to work out how they will split the rent, share chores, handle guests, and divide the bills before they move in together.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: Before we sign anything, I think we should agree on how we'll split everything fairly.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Definitely. The rent is the big one. Since your room is larger, maybe you pay a bit more?

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: That sounds reasonable. How about I cover sixty percent and you cover forty?

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Works for me. What about the bills — electricity, water, and the internet?

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: I'd suggest we split those right down the middle, regardless of room size.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Fair enough. We could set up a shared account and each transfer our share at the start of the month.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: Good idea. That way nobody has to chase the other one for money.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Now the tricky part — chores. I really don't want the kitchen turning into a disaster zone.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: Same here. Why don't we make a simple rota and swap tasks every week?

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Perfect. You take the bathroom one week, I take it the next, and we alternate the kitchen.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: And we each clean up after ourselves straight away. Dirty dishes piling up drives me crazy.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Agreed. There's one more thing I wanted to bring up — having guests over.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: I was about to mention that too. I'm fine with visitors, as long as you give me a heads-up.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Of course. And maybe we set a limit, like no one staying over for more than a few nights without asking.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: That feels respectful. As long as we communicate, I don't think guests will be a problem.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: I think we've covered everything. Honestly, this was a lot easier than I expected.

body.speaker: Megan
body.text: It really was. Let's write it all down so there are no misunderstandings later.

body.speaker: Daniel
body.text: Smart move. A clear agreement now saves a lot of arguments down the line.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Before we sign anything, I think we should agree on how we'll split everything fairly.
sentences.translationVi: Trước khi ký bất cứ thứ gì, tôi nghĩ chúng ta nên thống nhất cách chia mọi thứ một cách công bằng.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Definitely.
sentences.translationVi: Chắc chắn rồi.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: The rent is the big one.
sentences.translationVi: Tiền thuê nhà là khoản lớn nhất.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Since your room is larger, maybe you pay a bit more?
sentences.translationVi: Vì phòng của bạn lớn hơn, có lẽ bạn trả nhiều hơn một chút?

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: That sounds reasonable.
sentences.translationVi: Nghe có vẻ hợp lý.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: How about I cover sixty percent and you cover forty?
sentences.translationVi: Hay là tôi chịu sáu mươi phần trăm còn bạn chịu bốn mươi?

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Works for me.
sentences.translationVi: Được với tôi.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: What about the bills — electricity, water, and the internet?
sentences.translationVi: Còn các hóa đơn thì sao — điện, nước, và internet?

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: I'd suggest we split those right down the middle, regardless of room size.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi đề nghị chúng ta chia đều những khoản đó, bất kể kích thước phòng.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Fair enough.
sentences.translationVi: Cũng hợp lý.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: We could set up a shared account and each transfer our share at the start of the month.
sentences.translationVi: Chúng ta có thể lập một tài khoản chung và mỗi người chuyển phần của mình vào đầu tháng.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Good idea.
sentences.translationVi: Ý hay đấy.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: That way nobody has to chase the other one for money.
sentences.translationVi: Như vậy không ai phải đi đòi tiền người kia.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Now the tricky part — chores.
sentences.translationVi: Bây giờ đến phần khó — việc nhà.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: I really don't want the kitchen turning into a disaster zone.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi thực sự không muốn nhà bếp biến thành một khu vực thảm họa.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Same here.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi cũng vậy.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Why don't we make a simple rota and swap tasks every week?
sentences.translationVi: Sao chúng ta không lập một lịch phân công đơn giản và đổi việc mỗi tuần?

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Perfect.
sentences.translationVi: Tuyệt vời.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: You take the bathroom one week, I take it the next, and we alternate the kitchen.
sentences.translationVi: Bạn lo phòng tắm một tuần, tôi lo tuần tiếp theo, và chúng ta luân phiên nhà bếp.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: And we each clean up after ourselves straight away.
sentences.translationVi: Và mỗi người tự dọn dẹp ngay sau khi dùng xong.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Dirty dishes piling up drives me crazy.
sentences.translationVi: Bát đĩa bẩn chất đống khiến tôi phát điên.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Agreed.
sentences.translationVi: Đồng ý.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: There's one more thing I wanted to bring up — having guests over.
sentences.translationVi: Còn một việc nữa tôi muốn nêu ra — chuyện mời khách đến chơi.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: I was about to mention that too.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi cũng định nhắc đến chuyện đó.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: I'm fine with visitors, as long as you give me a heads-up.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi không phiền có khách, miễn là bạn báo trước cho tôi.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Of course.
sentences.translationVi: Tất nhiên rồi.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: And maybe we set a limit, like no one staying over for more than a few nights without asking.
sentences.translationVi: Và có lẽ chúng ta đặt một giới hạn, kiểu như không ai ở lại quá vài đêm mà không hỏi trước.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: That feels respectful.
sentences.translationVi: Điều đó nghe rất tôn trọng nhau.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: As long as we communicate, I don't think guests will be a problem.
sentences.translationVi: Miễn là chúng ta trao đổi với nhau, tôi không nghĩ khách khứa sẽ là vấn đề.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: I think we've covered everything.
sentences.translationVi: Tôi nghĩ chúng ta đã bàn hết mọi thứ.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Honestly, this was a lot easier than I expected.
sentences.translationVi: Thành thật mà nói, việc này dễ hơn nhiều so với tôi tưởng.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: It really was.
sentences.translationVi: Đúng là vậy thật.

sentences.speaker: Megan
sentences.text: Let's write it all down so there are no misunderstandings later.
sentences.translationVi: Hãy viết tất cả ra để sau này không có hiểu lầm.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: Smart move.
sentences.translationVi: Một nước đi thông minh.

sentences.speaker: Daniel
sentences.text: A clear agreement now saves a lot of arguments down the line.
sentences.translationVi: Một thỏa thuận rõ ràng ngay bây giờ sẽ tránh được nhiều tranh cãi về sau.

hintStarters.text: How about I cover … and you cover …
I'm fine with …, as long as you …
Why don't we make a simple rota and …

annotations.meaningVi: chia mọi thứ một cách công bằng
annotations.exampleEn: I think we should agree on how we'll split everything fairly.

annotations.meaningVi: khoản lớn nhất, vấn đề quan trọng nhất
annotations.exampleEn: The rent is the big one.
Since your room is larger, maybe you pay a bit more?
That sounds reasonable.
How about I cover sixty percent and you cover forty?

annotations.meaningVi: Được với tôi, tôi thấy ổn
annotations.exampleEn: Works for me.
I'd suggest we split those right down the middle.
We split those right down the middle, regardless of room size.
We could set up a shared account.

annotations.meaningVi: chuyển phần đóng góp của mình
annotations.exampleEn: Each transfer our share at the start of the month.

annotations.phrase: chase the other one for money
annotations.exampleEn: That way nobody has to chase the other one for money.

annotations.meaningVi: phần khó khăn, phần nan giải
annotations.exampleEn: Now the tricky part — chores.

annotations.meaningVi: khu vực bừa bộn, lộn xộn
annotations.exampleEn: I really don't want the kitchen turning into a disaster zone.
Why don't we make a simple rota and swap tasks every week?
I take it the next, and we alternate the kitchen.

annotations.meaningVi: tự dọn dẹp sau khi mình dùng
annotations.exampleEn: And we each clean up after ourselves straight away.
Dirty dishes piling up drives me crazy.
There's one more thing I wanted to bring up.
There's one more thing I wanted to bring up — having guests over.
And maybe we set a limit.
A clear agreement now saves a lot of arguments down the line.

grammarNotes.title: Making suggestions: 'How about …?' and 'Why don't we …?'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'How about + chủ ngữ + động từ?' hoặc 'Why don't we + động từ nguyên thể?' để đưa ra đề nghị một cách lịch sự. Ví dụ: 'How about I cover sixty percent?' và 'Why don't we make a simple rota?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'How about + subject + verb?' or 'Why don't we + base verb?' to make polite suggestions. Examples: 'How about I cover sixty percent?' and 'Why don't we make a simple rota?'

grammarNotes.title: 'As long as' for conditions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'as long as' để nêu một điều kiện cần thiết, có nghĩa là 'miễn là / chỉ cần'. Ví dụ: 'I'm fine with visitors, as long as you give me a heads-up.'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'as long as' to state a necessary condition, meaning 'provided that / only if'. Example: 'I'm fine with visitors, as long as you give me a heads-up.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: When sharing a home, is it better to write down every rule in advance, or to stay flexible and sort out problems as they come up?
